selected_topic: Rare civet cat released into habitat after rescue in Dera Ghazi Khan
news_angle: A rare Indian civet cat was rescued and released back into the wild in Pakistan, highlighting successful wildlife protection efforts.
why_this_is_news: This event is a fresh, specific wildlife rescue and release, featuring a rare species and positive conservation action.
specific_action: rescued and released into its natural habitat
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao as a daring urban explorer frees a rare wild friend back into the jungle with a confident, triumphant stance
image_prompt: A sleek, rare Indian civet cat in mid-release into lush green forest territory, with Hunk Mao the cool orange tabby cat standing nearby wearing a black hoodie, cap backwards, sunglasses pushed up, and a mischievous confident grin. Hunk Mao strikes an energetic, dynamic pose with one paw extended toward the civet, symbolizing freedom and adventure. The background is vivid and cinematic with sunlit trees and vibrant wildlife colors, capturing a heroic, stylish anime vibe blending urban streetwear with natural wilderness.
easter_eggs: Hunk Mao's collar tag shaped like a paw print with a tiny civet silhouette; a graffiti-style mural in the background showing civet footprints.
caption: A rare Indian civet cat was successfully rescued and released back into its natural habitat in Pakistan. Hunk Mao approves this wild comeback—freedom never looked so cool!
risk_notes: None. Content complies fully with allowed categories and editorial rules.
